records.ety: [L. invadere, invasum; pref. in- in + vadere to go, akin to E. wade: cf. OF. invader, F. envahir. See Wade.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Invaded; p. pr. & vb. n. Invading.]

defs: To go into or upon; to pass within the confines of; to enter; -- used of forcible or rude ingress.
quotes: Which becomes a body, and doth then invade The state of life, out of the grisly shade.

defs: To enter with hostile intentions; to enter with a view to conquest or plunder; to make an irruption into; to attack; as, the Romans invaded Great Britain.
quotes: Such an enemy Is risen to invade us.

defs: To attack; to infringe; to encroach on; to violate; as, the king invaded the rights of the people.
To grow or spread over; to affect injuriously and progressively; as, gangrene invades healthy tissue.
